title: Combinatorial Purged Cross-Validation (CPCV) - distribution of Sharpe paths
source: GitHub repo scout (4 Haiku explorers) + quant-phd-advisor literature review, 2026-05-31. Conversation: 'search quant trading repositories'.
why: Our walk-forward yields a SINGLE Sharpe path; CPCV yields a DISTRIBUTION of paths (phi = k*C(N,k)/N) which is exactly what DSR/PSR were designed to consume. Akyildirim et al. 2024 (synthetic) show CPCV gives lower PBO / higher DSR than WF, which is the WEAKEST overfit detector. quant-phd-advisor: strongest NEW validation information of the group — BUT it splits N groups combinatorially, reducing events-per-path, which AMPLIFIES our fold-starvation problem.
value_rationale: VERDICT: Adopt-with-caveats. High value + best literature support, but BLOCKED behind the fold-starvation fix and most useful on H4 (more events/group).
scope: Extend pipeline/walk_forward.py make_folds with the group-combination loop; reuse existing PurgedTimeSeriesSplit purge+embargo
CRITICAL: do NOT start until the <30-trade fold-starvation root cause is fixed — otherwise most CPCV paths are all-NaN and the path distribution is uninformative
Implement/validate on H4 first; sanity-check on ema_cross positive control (median ~= current single-path WF Sharpe, non-degenerate spread)
recommendation: Adopt-with-caveats, sequence AFTER the starvation fix. If starvation is fixed and folds reliably exceed ~50 trades, this becomes the single best validation instrument.
out_of_scope: Blocked until fold-starvation (see MEMORY project_stack_gate_starvation) is resolved. Premature on current sparse D1 folds.

external_refs.label: Akyildirim et al. 2024 — backtest overfitting CPCV vs WF
